sheet.description: You are blessed with an amazing technique to control Cursed Spirits.

description.p: Beginning at 1st level, you know the taste that no one will ever know, the one of curses. As an action or reaction for 1 Cursed Energy, you can exorcise a cursed spirit with 0 hit points, or a cursed spirit whose CR is less than your proficiency modifier within 120 feet of you that you can see and transform its soul into a small black orb for you to eat. After that, you can summon it as an ally to fight for you. You can summon your Cursed Spirits as an action by spending Cursed Energy equal to their CR. You can summon as many cursed spirits as part of your action equal to your Cursed Energy modifier. They lose their free will and follow each and every command you give them. If they are reduced to 0 hit points they die and cannot be revived by any means. They cannot use actions on their own. You can spend a bonus action to command each cursed spirit to attack, use their cursed techniques, or do something more specific, such as 'protect that creature, grab that, defend that area, etc. You can unsummon any number of cursed spirits as a bonus action, provided they are within line of sight.

You can only consume orbs without physical detriments if the creature consumed has a CR lower or equal to your level. If you try to eat an orb out of a creature with a CR higher than your level, you must roll a DC 5 + the creature's CR Constitution Saving Throw. On a success, you can absorb the creature and can use it after a short or long rest, on a failure you gain 2 levels of exhaustion and receive xd12 Necrotic Damage, X being the creature's CR.

You're also able to absorb other sorcerer's cursed spirits as long as you kill the master first. You can have as many cursed spirits as you want, but for every 10 cursed spirits you consume, you must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you will gain no aftereffects. On a failure, you will gain a level of exhaustion, you will gain two exhaustion levels if you fail the save by 5 or more, and you will gain three levels of exhaustion if you fail the save by 10 or more.

sheet.description: For 1 CE, exorcise a cursed spirit with 0 hit points or whose CR is less than {{proficiency}} within 120 feet of you that you can see, and transform its soul into a small black orb. You can consume orbs without punishment by succeeding on a DC (5 + the creature's CR) Constitution save or if the creature consumed has a CR lower than {{level}} and can use it after a short or long rest. On a failure you gain 2 levels of exhaustion and take Xd12 necrotic damage, X being the creature's CR. You're also able to absorb other sorcerer's cursed spirits as long as you kill the master first. As an action, you can summon up to {{ce:modifier}} Cursed Spirits by spending CE equal to their CR. They lose their free will and follow each and every command you give them. If they are reduced to 0 hit points they die and cannot be revived by any means. They cannot use actions on their own. As a bonus action, you can command each cursed spirit to attack, use their cursed techniques, or do something more specific. As a bonus action, you can unsummon any number of cursed spirits, provided they are within line of sight. You can have as many cursed spirits as you want, but for every 10 cursed spirits you consume, you must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, failure by 5 or more, or failure by 10 or more you gain 1, 2, or 3 levels of exhaustion respectively.

For 1 CE, exorcise a cursed spirit with 0 hit points or whose CR is less than {{proficiency}} within 120 feet of you that you can see, and transform its soul into a small black orb. You can consume orbs without punishment by succeeding on a DC ({{cursed:spirit:manipulation:absorption:dc}} + the creature's CR) Constitution save or if the creature consumed has a CR lower than {{level}} and can use it after a short or long rest. On a failure you gain 2 levels of exhaustion and take Xd12 necrotic damage, X being the creature's CR. You're also able to absorb other sorcerer's cursed spirits as long as you kill the master first. As an action or bonus action, you can summon up to {{ce:modifier}} Cursed Spirits by spending CE reduced by {{ce:modifier}} (min of 1) equal to their CR. They lose their free will and follow each and every command you give them. If they are reduced to 0 hit points they die and cannot be revived by any means. They cannot use actions on their own. As a bonus action, you can command each cursed spirit to attack, use their cursed techniques, or do something more specific. As a bonus action, you can unsummon any number of cursed spirits, provided they are within line of sight. You can have as many cursed spirits as you want, but for every 20 cursed spirits you consume, you must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, failure by 5 or more, or failure by 10 or more you gain 1, 2, or 3 levels of exhaustion respectively.

description.p: Once you reach 3rd level, your Cursed Energy increases the power of your Cursed Spirits in the following ways:

ul.li: Their Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores will gain a bonus equal to your Cursed Energy modifier.
They regain all HP at the end of a short rest.
They become immune to the frightened or charmed condition.
Regardless of distance, you'll know if your cursed spirits were exorcised.
In addition, as a reaction for x Cursed Energy you can sacrifice a cursed spirit in exchange for reducing incoming damage by xd20, with x being the CR of the creature sacrificed in the process.

sheet.description: Your Cursed Spirits' Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores increase by {{ce:modifier}}, regain all HP at the end of a short rest, and become immune to the frightened and charmed conditions. You know if your cursed spirits were exorcised regardless of distance, and can sacrifice a cursed spirit as a reaction for x cursed energy to reduce incoming damage by xd20 where x is the CR of the cursed spirit.

description.p: At 6th level, you have grown experienced with your cursed technique, giving you perfect control over your spirits. You will gain the following benefits:

ul.li: The amount of cursed spirits it takes for you to make a Wisdom saving throw is increased to 20.
Your cursed spirits cost is reduced by your Cursed Energy modifier (minimum cost of 1 Cursed Energy).
Your DC for eating cursed spirits with a CR higher than your level is decreased by your Cursed Energy modifier.
You can now summon cursed spirits as a bonus action.

description.p: When you reach 11th level, As an action you can put any number of your cursed spirits in a spiral-like pattern(minimum 2), making them into a dark spiral that you can use in an attack. Every creature in a 30-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw, on a success they take half damage, on a failure they take 2d12 Necrotic damage.
For every cursed spirit above 2, the area of effect will be increased by 5 feet, and the damage will increase by 1d12.

All of the cursed spirits that were used in the Uzumaki are killed. However, you can obtain the actions(including legendary actions)they have at a cost which must be paid when extracting them via Uzumaki. You can extract multiple actions from different curses as part of the process, but you must pay the cost for each action.
The cost for the actions is listed below and once extracted, the action can be used only once:

sheet.description: You can put any number of your cursed spirits into a spiral-like pattern(min of 2). Every creature in a 30-ft cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a success they take half damage, on a failure they take 2d12 Necrotic damage. For every cursed spirit above 2, the area of effect is increased by 5 ft and the damage increases by 1d12. All cursed spirits used in the Uzumaki are killed.

description.p: You can obtain the actions(including legendary actions)they have at a cost which must be paid when extracting them via Uzumaki. You can extract multiple actions from different curses as part of the process, but you must pay the cost for each action.
The cost for the actions is listed below and once extracted, the action can be used only once:

sheet.description: You can obtain the features of cursed spirits consumed in Maximum: Uzumaki by paying 3, 4, 5, or 10 CE for bonus actions, actions, reactions, and legendary actions respectively. You can extract multiple actions from different curses as part of the process, but you must pay the cost for each action. Once extracted, the action can be used only once.

description.p: Finally at 18th level, you have learned how to expand your domain. A tree of souls surrounded by four seated Buddhas at the cardinal points emerges behind you, as your barrier starts opening. The area targeted by this feature counts as difficult terrain for all creatures. You can use any of your cursed spirits without any cost. Cursed spirits within your domain must make a Charisma saving throw at the beginning of their turns or be instantly absorbed by you.

sheet.description: Your domain counts as difficult terrain for all creatures. You can use any of your cursed spirits without any cost. Cursed spirits within your domain must make a Charisma saving throw at the beginning of their turns or be instantly absorbed by you.
